question_set.question: A bank is interested in accounting information of a client for the purpose of

options.A: evaluating the entity's share of the market.
options.B: ascertaining the tax payable by the entity.
options.C: assessing the credit worthiness of the customer.
options.D: determining the dividend payable to shareholders.

question_set.explanation: Banks assess accounting information to evaluate the **credit worthiness of customers** to determine if they can repay loans.

question_set.question: An item recorded in the profit and loss account is
question_set.explanation: **General expenses** are recorded in the profit and loss account. Creditors, cash-in-hand, and debtors appear in the balance sheet.

question_set.question: The concept which assumes that transactions should be expressed using a common denominator is
question_set.explanation: The **money measurement concept** assumes all transactions are expressed in monetary terms (a common denominator) for accounting purposes.

question_set.question: The yearly depreciation charge using the straight line method is X−Y/Z. The letter X in the formula represents

options.C: estimated useful life of the asset.

question_set.explanation: In the straight-line depreciation formula, **X represents the cost of the asset**.

question_set.question: The yearly depreciation charge using the straight line method is X−Y/Z. The letter Z in the formula represents

options.C: estimated useful life of the asset.

question_set.explanation: In the straight-line depreciation formula, **Z represents the estimated useful life of the asset**.

question_set.question: In manufacturing account, wages of machine operators are classified as
question_set.explanation: Wages of machine operators are classified as **factory overheads** since they support the manufacturing process but aren't directly traceable to products.

question_set.question: Opening stock: Le 6,000, Sales: Le 180,000, Closing stock: Le 4,200, Mark-up is 33⅓%. The value of purchases is
question_set.explanation: With 33⅓% mark-up, cost of sales is Le 135,000. Purchases = COGS - Opening Stock + Closing Stock = 135,000 - 6,000 + 4,200 = **Le 133,200**.

question_set.question: Opening stock: Le 6,000, Sales: Le 180,000, Closing stock: Le 4,200, Mark-up is 33⅓%. The cost of goods sold is
question_set.explanation: With 33⅓% mark-up, profit = 1/4 of cost. Cost of goods sold = Sales ÷ (1 + 33⅓%) = 180,000 ÷ 1.333 = **Le 135,000**.

question_set.question: Opening stock: Le 6,000, Sales: Le 180,000, Closing stock: Le 4,200, Mark-up is 33⅓%. The gross profit is
question_set.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - COGS = 180,000 - 135,000 = **Le 45,000**.

question_set.question: A sole trader received the sum of ₦4,860 cash from a debtor. The effect of this transaction in the balance sheet is

options.A: decrease in stock and increase in debtor.
options.B: decrease in asset and increase in liability.
options.C: increase in cash and decrease in debtor.
options.D: increase in cash and decrease in capital.

question_set.explanation: Receiving cash from a debtor **increases cash** and **decreases debtors**. Total assets remain unchanged.

question_set.question: According to the entity concept, ownership is

options.A: vested on the board of directors.

question_set.explanation: The **entity concept** separates ownership from the business. The business is treated as a distinct entity separate from its owners.

question_set.question: An item recorded in the trial balance is
question_set.explanation: **Opening stock** is recorded in the trial balance. Closing stock, accrued expenses, and depreciation are adjustments made after the trial balance.

question_set.question: The class of shares which are available only to the promoters of a company are
question_set.explanation: **Deferred shares** are available only to the promoters of a company as a reward for their role in establishing the company.

question_set.question: Expense incurred but not yet paid is
question_set.explanation: An expense incurred but not yet paid is an **accrued expense**, classified as a **current liability** on the balance sheet.

question_set.question: Work-in-progress is an item in
question_set.explanation: **Work-in-progress** appears in the **manufacturing account** as it represents partially completed goods in the production process.

question_set.question: In an incomplete record, the excess of opening capital over closing capital is
question_set.explanation: If opening capital exceeds closing capital, it indicates a **loss** for the period (assuming no drawings or additional capital).

question_set.question: Bayo issued a credit note to Ayo who returned defective goods. Ayo will record this transaction on the

options.A: debit side of the sales ledger control account.
options.B: credit side of the sales ledger control account.
options.C: debit side of the purchases ledger control account.
options.D: credit side of the purchases ledger control account.

question_set.explanation: Ayo received a credit note from Bayo (a supplier), so Ayo records it on the **credit side of the purchases ledger control account**.

question_set.question: Bayo issued a credit note to Ayo who returned defective goods. Bayo will record this transaction on the

options.A: debit side of the sales ledger control account.
options.B: credit side of the sales ledger control account.
options.C: debit side of the purchases ledger control account.
options.D: credit side of the purchases ledger control account.

question_set.explanation: Bayo issued the credit note (as a seller), so Bayo records it on the **debit side of the sales ledger control account**.

question_set.question: The concept that underlies the comparing of expenditure for a period with the revenue of same period is
question_set.explanation: The **matching concept** ensures expenses are matched with revenues of the same period for accurate profit determination.

question_set.question: An item is classified as a current asset if it is

options.B: expected to be realized within the balance sheet date.

question_set.explanation: A **current asset** is expected to be converted to cash or realized **within one year** (or the normal operating cycle).

question_set.question: Subscriptions classified in the balance sheet as a current asset is subscriptions
question_set.explanation: **Subscriptions in advance** are classified as a **current liability** (not asset). However, subscriptions **in arrears** would be a current asset as they're receivable.

question_set.question: Which of the following items will not be charged to the manufacturing account?
question_set.explanation: **Warehouse rent** is not charged to the manufacturing account as it relates to storage after production. Other items are manufacturing costs.

question_set.question: An account in the real ledger is
question_set.explanation: **Machinery** is a real asset account in the real ledger. Sales, insurance, and discount received are nominal accounts.

question_set.question: The margin for a business is 2/5 and the cost of sales is $120,000. The mark-up for the business is
question_set.explanation: Margin = 2/5 means profit = 2/5 of sales. If margin = 2/5, then mark-up = margin/(1-margin) = (2/5)/(3/5) = **2/3**.

question_set.question: The margin for a business is 2/5 and the cost of sales is $120,000. The sales value for the business is
question_set.explanation: If margin = 2/5, then profit = 2/5 of sales. Cost = 3/5 of sales. So $120,000 = 3/5 × Sales. Sales = $120,000 × 5/3 = **$200,000**.

question_set.question: The objective of preparing a departmental account is to

options.A: ascertain the cost of raw materials of each department.
options.B: ascertain the profit of each department.
options.C: determine the value of unused stock of each department.
options.D: calculate the stock turnover ratio of each department.

question_set.explanation: The main objective of preparing a **departmental account** is to **ascertain the profit/loss of each department** separately.

question_set.question: A debit balance of D 61,000 on a customer's bank statement means that

options.A: the customer's bank balance is D 61,000.
options.B: the customer owes the bank D 61,000.
options.C: a customer's cheque of D 61,000 is yet to be cleared by the bank.
options.D: a direct deposit of D 61,000 was made by a customer.

question_set.explanation: A **debit balance** on the bank statement means the customer's account is **overdrawn** — the customer owes the bank D 61,000.

question_set.question: Trade creditors 1/1/2022: GH¢ 35,000, Payment to suppliers in 2022: GH¢ 30,000, Discounts received: GH¢ 1,800, Trade creditors 31/12/2022: GH¢ 24,000. The purchases for 2022 is
question_set.explanation: Purchases = Payments + Closing Creditors - Opening Creditors - Discounts = 30,000 + 24,000 - 35,000 - 1,800 = **GH¢ 20,800**.

question_set.question: Trade creditors 1/1/2022: GH¢ 35,000, Payment to suppliers in 2022: GH¢ 30,000, Discounts received: GH¢ 1,800, Trade creditors 31/12/2022: GH¢ 24,000. The balance sheet as at 31/12/2022 will show current liability of
question_set.explanation: The **current liability** shown on the balance sheet as at 31/12/2022 is the **closing trade creditors of GH¢ 24,000**.

question_set.question: A credit purchase of D 63 from Kofi has been entered in the accounts as D 16. This is an error of
question_set.explanation: This is an **error of original entry** — the transaction was recorded with the wrong amount (D 16 instead of D 63) at the source.

question_set.question: Taiyelolu and Ejire are partners sharing profits/losses equally. Capital accounts (1/1/2023): Taiyelolu ₦200,000, Ejire ₦100,000. Annual salaries: Taiyelolu ₦28,000, Ejire ₦24,000. Interest on capital is 10% and net profit is ₦120,000. The interest on Taiyelolu's capital is
question_set.explanation: Interest on Taiyelolu's capital = 10% × ₦200,000 = **₦20,000**.

question_set.question: Taiyelolu and Ejire are partners sharing profits/losses equally. Capital accounts (1/1/2023): Taiyelolu ₦200,000, Ejire ₦100,000. Annual salaries: Taiyelolu ₦28,000, Ejire ₦24,000. Interest on capital is 10% and net profit is ₦120,000. Ejire's share of profit is

Taiyelolu and Ejire are partners sharing profits/losses equally. Capital accounts (1/1/2023): Taiyelolu ₦200,000, Ejire ₦100,000. Drawings: Taiyelolu ₦20,000, Ejire ₦30,000. Annual salaries: Taiyelolu ₦28,000, Ejire ₦24,000. Interest on capital is 10% and net profit is ₦120,000. Taiyelolu's balance of current account at the end of the year is
question_set.explanation: Current account = Salary + Interest + Share of profit - Drawings = 28,000 + 20,000 + 19,000 - 20,000 = **₦47,000** (or check opening ₦40,000 + additions - drawings).

question_set.question: Purchased goodwill is purchase consideration less the value of
question_set.explanation: **Purchased goodwill** = Purchase price - Fair value of **net identifiable assets** (assets minus liabilities).

question_set.question: Errors in the trial balance are corrected using the
question_set.explanation: Errors found in the trial balance are corrected through **journal entries** to adjust the ledger accounts.

question_set.question: The equivalent of income and expenditure account in a limited liability company is
question_set.explanation: In a limited liability company, the **profit and loss account** is the equivalent of the **income and expenditure account** used in non-profit organizations.

question_set.question: The owner's claim on the asset of a business is
question_set.explanation: The owner's claim on the assets of a business is represented by **capital** (owner's equity).

question_set.question: A partner who has not contributed any form of capital into the firm but only lends his name to be used as a partner is a
question_set.explanation: A **nominal partner** lends only their name to the firm without contributing capital or taking active part in management.

question_set.question: Liquid assets refer to

options.A: cash and other assets that can be easily converted into cash.

question_set.explanation: **Liquid assets** are assets that can be **quickly converted to cash**, including cash, marketable securities, and receivables.

question_set.question: Ade bought goods worth Le 30,000 from Ode and was given a 7.5% discount. The evidence of payment for the goods Ade bought is the
question_set.explanation: The **receipt** is the evidence of payment for goods purchased. An invoice is a request for payment before payment is made.

question_set.question: Ade bought goods worth Le 30,000 from Ode and was given a 7.5% discount. The amount paid for the goods is
The proportion of the issued capital to be paid later is
question_set.explanation: **Uncalled-up capital** is the portion of issued capital that shareholders have not yet been called upon to pay.

question_set.question: One of the columns in a single column cash book is
question_set.explanation: **Particulars** is a column in all cash books showing details of transactions. A single column cash book has: Particulars, Debit, Credit columns.

question_set.question: Purchases of motor van: D 370,000, Building of laboratories: D 760,000, Purchase of drugs: D 110,000, Electricity: D 26,000, Repair of equipment: D 10,000. The amount of recurrent expenditure is
question_set.explanation: **Recurrent (revenue) expenditure** = Electricity + Repair of equipment = 26,000 + 10,000 = **D 36,000**. (Note: Drugs D 110,000 could also be recurrent)

question_set.question: Purchases of motor van: D 370,000, Building of laboratories: D 760,000, Purchase of drugs: D 110,000, Electricity: D 26,000, Repair of equipment: D 10,000. The capital expenditure is
question_set.explanation: **Capital expenditure** = Motor van + Building + Drugs = 370,000 + 760,000 + 110,000 = **D 1,240,000**. (Or D 870,000 if drugs excluded)

question_set.question: Shares which are issued at no cost to existing shareholders in proportion to existing shares held are
question_set.explanation: **Bonus shares** are shares issued free to existing shareholders in proportion to their current shareholding.

question_set.question: Which of the following items will be recorded on the credit side of a club's income and expenditure account?
question_set.explanation: **Donations received** are income items recorded on the **credit side** of the income and expenditure account.

question_set.question: Where the head office maintains all books of account, the cost of goods returned is credited to
question_set.explanation: When goods are returned, the cost is credited to the **Goods Sent to Branch Account** to reverse the original transfer.

question_set.question: Distribution of dividends to shareholders are made from profit
question_set.explanation: Dividends are distributed from profit **after tax** has been paid by the company.

question_set.question: The debit side of a trial balance total is GH¢ 2,000 more than the credit side. Which of the following errors would account for the difference?

options.A: A receipt of GH¢ 2,000 for rent has been omitted from the books.
options.B: Carriage inwards of GH¢ 2,000 has been debited to carriage outwards account.
options.C: Petty cash balance of GH¢ 1,000 has been omitted from the books.
options.D: Discount received of GH¢ 1,000 has been debited to discount allowed account.

question_set.explanation: Discount received should be credited (right account). Debiting it instead creates a swing of GH¢ 2,000 (should be -GH¢1,000 but is +GH¢1,000), making debit side GH¢2,000 higher.
